summary.name: Asset Growth Anomaly LongShort Equity
summary.author: Gurumeher Sawhney

summary.description_full:
The strategy is a yearly cross sectional equity factor trade that exploits the historical tendency for firms with aggressively expanding balance sheets to subsequently underperform, while firms with weak asset growth tend to outperform. Each year it ranks a broad universe of US listed common stocks using the year over year percentage change in total assets from financial statements, then implements a dollar neutral long short portfolio that is long the lowest asset growth decile and short the highest asset growth decile, targeting the asset growth anomaly and related overinvestment/empire building effects.

The investable universe is US equities on NYSE, NASDAQ, or AMEX with valid fundamental data and positive total assets, excluding certain industry templates. The ranking signal is refreshed annually at the start of the year using fundamental updates; a two year warmup ensures two annual asset snapshots are available. Rebalancing occurs once per year before the market open, equal weighting names within each side, with 50 percent gross long exposure and 50 percent gross short exposure (net approximately zero). No explicit volatility, drawdown, or stop loss overlay is applied beyond diversification and annual turnover.

raw.name: Asset Growth Anomaly LongShort Equity

author.name: Gurumeher Sawhney
